fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Arizona Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Arizona - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity arizona, how to transfer physical therapist license to arizona, physical therapist compact arizona, arizona physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Arizona Board of Physical Therapy
board.parent_dept: Arizona does not mandate state dietitian licensure. The national CDR credential is the recognized standard.

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Arizona.

steps.title: Create Account & Start Application
steps.description: Register and create a user account on the Arizona State Board of Physical Therapy Online Licensing System (https://licensing.az.gov/). Log in, select 'Apply for a New License,' and choose 'Physical Therapist' from the available options.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application, providing personal, educational, and professional history. Ensure all mandatory fields are completed accurately within the Arizona State Board of Physical Therapy Online Licensing System.

steps.title: Submit Required Documents
steps.description: Upload necessary supporting documents, such as official transcripts from your physical therapy program, directly through the portal. Arrange for any required verification forms or letters to be sent to the Board.

steps.title: Arrange for NPTE Scores & Background Check
steps.description: Have your official National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE) scores sent directly to the Arizona State Board of Physical Therapy. Complete and submit the required fingerprint-based background check as instructed within your application or on the Board's website.

steps.title: Pay Application Fees
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable application fee and any other associated licensing fees through the secure payment system within the Arizona State Board of Physical Therapy Online Licensing System.

steps.title: Board Review & Licensure
steps.description: Monitor your application status within your online account. The Board will review your complete application and all submitted documentation. Once approved, your license will be issued and reflected in the online verification system.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Arizona physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $100. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Arizona have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Arizona participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Arizona without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Arizona?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 4 to 6 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Arizona while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Arizona
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Arizona, applicants must create an account and submit their application through the Arizona State Board of Physical Therapy Online Licensing System.

official_links.portal_name: Arizona State Board of Physical Therapy Online Licensing System
official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the Arizona State Board of Physical Therapy Online Licensing System, click 'Register for an Account' (if you don't have one), and follow the prompts to create your user profile with an email address and password. Verify your email to complete registration.
